svg.title: Of Virtualization: Virtualization Structure/Tools and Mechanisms

text.data-narration: Hypervisor: The software that manages the virtualization process and provides a layer of abstraction between the physical hardware and the virtual machines. Types of Hypervisors: Type 1 and Type 2 Hypervisors. Examples: VMware ESXi, Microsoft Hyper-V, KVM
Virtual Machines: The software environments that run on top of the hypervisor.
Management Layer: The layer that manages the virtualization process and provides a layer of abstraction between the hypervisor and the virtual machines.

Hypervisor Types: Type 1 and Type 2 Hypervisors. Type 1 Hypervisor: Runs directly on the physical hardware. Type 2 Hypervisor: Runs on top of an existing operating system. Examples: VMware ESXi, Microsoft Hyper-V, KVM

Virtualization: The process of creating a virtual environment, which is a software emulation of a physical hardware system. This allows multiple virtual machines (VMs) to run on a single physical host, increasing hardware utilization and improving system efficiency. Types of Virtualization: Hardware Virtualization and Software Virtualization. Examples: VMware, Microsoft Hyper-V, KVM

Conclusion: Virtualization is a powerful tool for increasing hardware utilization and improving system efficiency. It allows multiple VMs to run on a single physical host, making it an essential technology for modern computing. By understanding the different types of virtualization and how they work, you can unlock the full potential of your hardware and improve your computing experience.
